mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signal expanding labor income and aggregate demand, reducing near-term recession risk and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC for PAYEMS changes falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive PAYEMS changes do not coincide with positive 20-day returns (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) above 2% indicates restrictive monetary policy; further increases in this rate raise discount rates and debt-service costs, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.0% and is rising.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in consumer prices (CPIAUCSL) and contraction in industrial production (INDPRO) produces a stagflationary risk premium that equity markets reprice downward over the next 60 trading days, an effect neither variable captures independently.
failure_condition: The interaction term between CPIAUCSL changes and INDPRO changes generates a Rank IC below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in retail sales (RSAFS) captures real-time consumer spending momentum and correlates positively with near-term corporate revenue surprises, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: RSAFS increases fail to predict positive 5-day S&P 500 returns, with Rank IC below 0.03 over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) falling below 1,200 thousand units signal deteriorating residential investment and forward-looking demand weakness, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 55% of instances when HOUST is below 1,200, rejecting the demand-contraction channel.

mechanism_narrative: Jointly rising unemployment (UNRATE) and declining federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS) indicate a reactive monetary easing in response to labor market deterioration, amplifying recession expectations and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal from UNRATE and FEDFUNDS produces a walk-forward Rank IC below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in > 50% of joint-signal instances.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP (GDPC1) confirms broad-based aggregate demand expansion, supporting corporate earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth fails to correlate with positive 60-day S&P 500 returns, with Rank IC below 0.03 over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Annualized consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) exceeding 2.5% triggers fears of persistent monetary tightening and higher real discount rates, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns are non-negative in more than 50% of instances when CPIAUCSL YoY exceeds 2.5%, falsifying the inflation-risk-premium channel.
